name: Esther's Bracelets
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat the eggs (or egg substitute) until fluffy and lemon-colored.
3. Continue beating, adding the sugar, oil, and then the orange juice.
4. Stir in the vanilla extract.
5. In another bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt (if used). Mix well.
6. Add this dry mixture to the beaten egg mixture. Stir thoroughly until a smooth dough forms.
7. Spray a cookie sheet with vegetable oil spray.
8. Divide the dough into 4 parts. Shape one part into a log about 13 inches long and 1 inch wide.
9. Cut the log into 13 pieces. Roll each piece into a 7-inch rope.
10. Lay each rope on the cookie sheet, forming it into a closed circle to create a bracelet-shaped cookie.
11. Cover the junction with a candied cherry or a whole almond dipped in the beaten egg white.
12. Repeat with the remaining pieces of dough.
13. Bake for 15 minutes. Do not brown.
14. Cool on racks and store in an air-tight container. May be frozen. Makes 52 cookies.
Nutrition analysis per cookie (with eggs), not including the "clasps":
Calories: 58
Protein: 1g
Carbohydrates: 8g
Fat: 2.4g
Cholesterol: 8mg
Sodium: 23mg
